items.title: ChatLoopBackOff Episode 77: bootc with Henrik Rexed
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>In this episode of ChatLoopBackOff, CNCF Ambassador Henrik Rexed explores bootc, a CNCF project that reimagines the operating system as a container image.

bootc brings the "build once, run anywhere" container workflow to the host OS. By using OCI-compliant images to manage the entire system, it allows platform teams to handle OS updates and security patches through the same registries and CI/CD pipelines used for applications. It’s a transactional, atomic approach designed to eliminate configuration drift and enable true GitOps for infrastructure.

Henrik will take a hands-on look at how bootc works with live exploration and an honest assessment of its role in modern platform engineering.

Whether you're into immutable infrastructure, edge computing, or streamlining Linux lifecycles, join us for this practical dive into bootc!</article>

items.title: Keynote: The Cloud Native Feedback Loop... Karena Angell, Katie Gamanji, Chad Beaudin & Ahmed Bebars
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Mumbai, India (18-19 June, 2026), Yokohama, Japan (29-30 July, 2026), and Shanghai, China (8-9 September, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Keynote: The Cloud Native Feedback Loop: How End Users and Developers Drive CNCF Projects Forward - Karena Angell, Red Hat; Katie Gamanji, Apple; Chad Beaudin, Boeing Software Factory; Ahmed Bebars, The New York Times

How do today’s reference architectures become real, production platforms built on CNCF projects that evolve, mature, and earn long-term trust?

In this joint keynote, the CNCF End User Technical Advisory Board (TAB) and Technical Oversight Committee (TOC) cover the development and adoption perspectives, mapping reference architectures and real-world production needs to the project lifecycle decisions that shape the CNCF ecosystem. Along the way, they connect this technical and governance journey to the data and tools that support smarter decisions at scale, including LFX and LF Insights, showing how contributor health, adoption signals, and ecosystem metrics increasingly guide both end-user platform strategy and project stewardship.</article>

items.title: Cloud Native Live: Wiring together the CNCF Ecosystem for the Enterprise
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content: <article>What actually sits under the hood of a "production-ready" container orchestration solution? We’re bypassing the slides to live-demo the integration of the "fan wheel" of components—including ingress, policy control, and authentication—necessary for enterprise scale. Witness a transparent look at how disparate open source tools interface to create a secure, manageable, and resilient platform for modern applications.</article>

items.title: Day 3 - KubeCon + CloudNativeCon 2026 highlights from Amsterdam
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Experience the energy of KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe in Amsterdam! This video captures the highlights of an incredible week spent with the open source community.

Discover why attending this event is a game-changing experience for professionals:
* Connect with Peers: Take advantage of the unique chance to network with fellow engineers from around the world.
* Meet the Experts: Get face-to-face time with the actual maintainers and developers behind the tools and technologies you use every day.
* Stay Ahead of the Curve: Learn about the latest ideas emerging in the open-source community and gain insights into where the industry is moving.

As we wrap up the final day, we want to thank everyone who joined us. Let’s keep the cloud native movement progressing together!</article>

items.title: Day 2 - KubeCon + CloudNativeCon 2026 highlights from Amsterdam
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Join us for highlights from day two of KubeCon + CloudNativeCon in Amsterdam!
* Community Engagement: One of the primary goals of attending is to connect directly with the community.
* Valuable Insights: This event provides an opportunity for meaningful conversations with the individuals who use and touch the technology every single day.
* Passion for Learning: Attendees are driven by a genuine desire to learn and shared knowledge.
* Building the Future: This is more than just an industry event; it is a unique collaborative system focused on building the future of technology.

Get Involved! We encourage everyone to come along, talk to maintainers, and get involved with the community.</article>

items.title: Day 1 - KubeCon + CloudNativeCon 2026 highlights from Amsterdam
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Experience the highlights from day one of KubeCon + CloudNativeCon 2026 in Amsterdam! This year marks the largest KubeCon event to date, bringing together a massive community of cloud native enthusiasts.

You will always find:
* Record-Breaking Scale: With over 13,500 attendees, this is the biggest KubeCon + CloudNativeCon event yet!
* Expert Insights: Attendees have the unique opportunity to learn directly from industry experts in cloud native architectures.
* Real-World Knowledge: The event provides a platform to understand how technologies work through others' perspectives and real-life experiences.
* Collaborative Community: Join thousands of professionals as they connect, ask questions, and keep the cloud-native movement moving forward.</article>

items.title: Day 0 - KubeCon + CloudNativeCon 2026 highlights from Amsterdam
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content: <article>KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe returned to Amsterdam! Day zero featured lightning talks and co-located events, and we hope CNCF Maintainers enjoyed Sunday's largest Maintainer Summit yet!</article>

items.title: Sponsored Keynote: From Pilot to Production: Scaling and Optimizing Agen... Idit Levine & Keith Babo
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Mumbai, India (18-19 June, 2026), Yokohama, Japan (29-30 July, 2026), and Shanghai, China (8-9 September, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Sponsored Keynote: From Pilot to Production: Scaling and Optimizing Agentic Workloads on Kubernetes - Idit Levine, Founder & CEO, Solo.io & Keith Babo, Chief Product Officer, Solo.io

Kubernetes and the CNCF ecosystem are rapidly evolving to support agent-native workloads, with open-source projects like agentgateway and kagent extending Kubernetes to treat agents, tools, and skills as first-class citizens. With this foundation in place, platform teams can bring agentic workloads to production. But getting to production is just the beginning. The real challenge is what happens when you scale - moving beyond a few specialized teams getting initial projects to production to every developer in your organization integrating with agents, tools, and skills as part of their core workflow.

This talk will cover the concrete infrastructure patterns emerging in the community for discovery, governance, and operational excellence as agentic workloads move from pilot to enterprise scale.</article>

items.title: Sponsored Keynote: Scaling Platform Ops with AI Agents: Troubleshootin... Jorge Palma & Natan Yellin
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Mumbai, India (18-19 June, 2026), Yokohama, Japan (29-30 July, 2026), and Shanghai, China (8-9 September, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Sponsored Keynote: Scaling Platform Ops with AI Agents: Troubleshooting to Remediation - Jorge Palma, Principal PDM Manager, Microsoft & Natan Yellin, CEO, Robusta

This keynote explores the practical reality of deploying AI agents to maintain Kubernetes clusters at scale. We'll demonstrate HolmesGPT, an open-source CNCF sandbox project that connects LLMs to operational and observability data to diagnose production issues. You'll see how agents reduce MTTR by correlating logs, metrics, and cluster state far faster than manual investigation.

Then we'll tackle the harder problem: moving from diagnosis to remediation. We'll show how agents with remediation policies can detect and fix issues autonomously, within strict RBAC boundaries, approval workflows, and audit trails. We'll be honest about challenges: LLM non-determinism, building trust, and why guardrails are non-negotiable.

This isn't about replacing SREs; it's about multiplying their effectiveness so they can focus on creative problem-solving and system design.</article>

items.title: Keynote: Orchestrating Document Data Extraction with Dapr Agents - Fabian Steinbach
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Mumbai, India (18-19 June, 2026), Yokohama, Japan (29-30 July, 2026), and Shanghai, China (8-9 September, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Keynote: Orchestrating Document Data Extraction with Dapr Agents - Fabian Steinbach, Software Architect, ZEISS

Extracting structured data from highly variable, multi-lingual documents with different layouts and handwriting is a complex challenge. At ZEISS Vision Care, we needed a reliable way to process these documents without sacrificing performance or accuracy. We solved this by building resilient data extraction processes using Dapr Agents. Adopting a vendor-neutral, cloud-native framework allowed us to build highly deterministic workflows that seamlessly blend specialized OCR, general LLM calls, and standard code. This architecture ensures reliable execution while giving us the flexibility to swap AI providers and go from concept to production in just two months.</article>

items.title: Sponsored Keynote: From Complexity to Clarity: Engineering an Invisible Kubernetes - Jesse Butler
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Mumbai, India (18-19 June, 2026), Yokohama, Japan (29-30 July, 2026), and Shanghai, China (8-9 September, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Sponsored Keynote: From Complexity to Clarity: Engineering an Invisible Kubernetes - Jesse Butler, Principal Product Manager and Technologist, Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)

Kubernetes has become the ubiquitous control plane for some of the most demanding distributed workloads ever built: AI training and inference at scale, heterogeneous and accelerated compute, and complex multi-tenant platforms. Yet even as it becomes the standard, developers still reason about infrastructure details while platform teams translate application intent into cluster configuration, autoscaling, and resource tuning across thousands of services. If Kubernetes is to truly fade into the stack, the model must evolve. Infrastructure must respond dynamically to workload signals in real time. Higher-level abstractions must reduce direct exposure to low-level primitives without sacrificing control. Governance must be programmatic and consistent across distributed environments. This keynote traces that evolution through three community-driven upstream innovations, Karpenter, kro, and Cedar, and the engineering choices shaping Kubernetes for its next chapter.</article>
